title: Bump github.com/uber/jaeger-client-go from 2.16.0+incompatible to 2.19.0+incompatible
body:
Bumps [github.com/uber/jaeger-client-go](https://github.com/uber/jaeger-client-go) from 2.16.0+incompatible to 2.19.0+incompatible.
Release notes *Sourced from [github.com/uber/jaeger-client-go's releases](https://github.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/releases).* > ## Release 2.19.0 > - Upgrade jaeger-lib to 2.2 and Prom client to 1.x ([#434](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/434)) -- Yuri Shkuro > > ## Release 2.18.1 > - Remove go.mod / go.sum that interfere with `go get` ([#432](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/432)) > > ## Release 2.18.0 > * Add option "noDebugFlagOnForcedSampling" for tracer initialization ([#423](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/423)) > > ## Release 2.17.0 > - Add a flag for firehose mode ([#419](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/419)) > - Default sampling server URL to agent ([#414](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/414)) > - Update default sampling rate when sampling strategy is refreshed ([#413](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/413)) > - Support "Self" Span Reference ([#411](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/411)) > - Don't complain about blank service name if tracing is Disabled ([#410](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/410)) Yuri > - Use IP address from tag if exist ([#402](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/402)) > - Expose span data to custom reporters [fixes [#394](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/394)] ([#399](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/399)) > - Fix the span allocation in the pool ([#381](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/381))
Changelog *Sourced from [github.com/uber/jaeger-client-go's changelog](https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-go/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md).* > Changes by Version > ================== > > 2.19.1 (unreleased) > ------------------- > > - Nothing yet. > > > 2.19.0 (2019-09-23) > ------------------- > > - Upgrade jaeger-lib to 2.2 and unpin Prom client ([#434](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/434)) -- Yuri Shkuro > > > 2.18.1 (2019-09-16) > ------------------- > > - Remove go.mod / go.sum that interfere with `go get` ([#432](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/432)) > > > 2.18.0 (2019-09-09) > ------------------- > > - Add option "noDebugFlagOnForcedSampling" for tracer initialization [resolves [#422](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/422)] ([#423](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/423)) > > > 2.17.0 (2019-08-30) > ------------------- > > - Add a flag for firehose mode ([#419](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/419)) > - Default sampling server URL to agent ([#414](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/414)) > - Update default sampling rate when sampling strategy is refreshed ([#413](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/413)) > - Support "Self" Span Reference ([#411](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/411)) > - Don't complain about blank service name if tracing is Disabled ([#410](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/410)) Yuri > - Use IP address from tag if exist ([#402](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/402)) > - Expose span data to custom reporters [fixes [#394](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/394)] ([#399](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/399)) > - Fix the span allocation in the pool ([#381](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/381)) > > > 2.16.0 (2019-03-24) > ------------------- > > - Add baggage to B3 codec ([#319](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/319)) > - Add support for 128bit trace ids to zipkin thrift spans. ([#378](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/378)) > - Update zipkin propagation logic to support 128bit traceIDs ([#373](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/373)) > - Accept "true" for the x-b3-sampled header ([#356](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/356)) > > - Allow setting of PoolSpans from Config object ([#322](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/322)) > - Make propagators public to allow wrapping ([#379](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/379)) > ... (truncated)
Commits - [`d4d621c`](https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-go/commit/d4d621cd6ed9aa5f1fc91eb0d0211d336bd6b79d) Prepare release 2.19.0 - [`e95cd44`](https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-go/commit/e95cd44c656dbc4f5704e522fbaa178e5969f8eb) Upgrade jaeger-lib to 2.2 and unpin Prom client ([#434](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/434)) - [`f349093`](https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-go/commit/f3490930ed601678f761887847492801a299af73) Back to development - [`9a8d51c`](https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-go/commit/9a8d51c6717212c22073c8a3f8fe6a69d13559f8) Prepare release 2.18.1 - [`f188b2e`](https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-go/commit/f188b2ed04b88766c82731c2f923e681eec5a32d) Remove Go module files ([#432](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/432)) - [`5507876`](https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-go/commit/55078767ae88c27804899a9ca5e6ef835a0df45d) Back to development ([#429](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/429)) - [`258182f`](https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-go/commit/258182f41c1950c24e0acd5407abd810b6174d87) Preparing for release 2.18.0 ([#428](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/428)) - [`c927455`](https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-go/commit/c927455062c8f9570c6109ff1dc283c89dcbebc6) Pass USE_DEP to make ([#426](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/426)) - [`0ba3a02`](https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-go/commit/0ba3a027993746a337cd174eaac31620589f1867) Remove go modules ([#425](https://github-redirect.dependabot.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/issues/425)) - [`3434ad5`](https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-go/commit/3434ad534caed2556fb03ff2c666551ddc075360) Add "sampleWithoutDebugFlag" option for tracer to decide set/not set debug sp... - Additional commits viewable in [compare view](https://github.com/uber/jaeger-client-go/compare/v2.16.0...v2.19.0)

[![Dependabot compatibility score](https://api.dependabot.com/badges/compatibility_score?dependency-name=github.com/uber/jaeger-client-go&package-manager=go_modules&previous-version=2.16.0+incompatible&new-version=2.19.0+incompatible)](https://dependabot.com/compatibility-score.html?dependency-name=github.com/uber/jaeger-client-go&package-manager=go_modules&previous-version=2.16.0+incompatible&new-version=2.19.0+incompatible) Dependabot will resolve any conflicts with this PR as long as you don't alter it yourself. You can also trigger a rebase manually by commenting `@dependabot rebase`. [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-start) [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-end) --- **Note:** This repo was added to Dependabot recently, so you'll receive a maximum of 5 PRs for your first few update runs. Once an update run creates fewer than 5 PRs we'll remove that limit. You can always request more updates by clicking `Bump now` in your [Dependabot dashboard](https://app.dependabot.com).
Dependabot commands and options
You can trigger Dependabot actions by commenting on this PR: - `@dependabot rebase` will rebase this PR - `@dependabot recreate` will recreate this PR, overwriting any edits that have been made to it - `@dependabot merge` will merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot squash and merge` will squash and merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot cancel merge` will cancel a previously requested merge and block automerging - `@dependabot reopen` will reopen this PR if it is closed - `@dependabot close` will close this PR and stop Dependabot recreating it. You can achieve the same result by closing it manually - `@dependabot ignore this major version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this major version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this minor version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this minor version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this dependency` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this dependency (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot use these labels` will set the current labels as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot use these reviewers` will set the current reviewers as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot use these assignees` will set the current assignees as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot use this milestone` will set the current milestone as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot badge me` will comment on this PR with code to add a "Dependabot enabled" badge to your readme Additionally, you can set the following in your Dependabot [dashboard](https://app.dependabot.com): - Update frequency (including time of day and day of week) - Pull request limits (per update run and/or open at any time) - Out-of-range updates (receive only lockfile updates, if desired) - Security updates (receive only security updates, if desired) Finally, you can contact us by mentioning @dependabot.

comments.body:
Hi @dependabot-preview[bot]. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) or [openshift](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
@vrothberg @mheon, anyway to set it so dependabot doesn't need and ok-to-test to be chunked in by one of us?

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *dependabot-preview[bot]*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> @vrothberg @mheon, anyway to set it so dependabot doesn't need and ok-to-test to be chunked in by one of us?

As we have to manually run `make vendor` and update the commit, the ok-to-test is actually useful since it prevents the entire CI from running and wasting energy - the vendor checks will fail in most cases.
Ah, thanks @vrothberg, I thought the dependabot was doing the vendor too,.
